title: GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) Compliance Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: GDPR Art.46(2)(c) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.46(2)(c) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.1.37: GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) Compliance Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.1.37' running on gdpr_scc_register.csv, gdpr_processor_register.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup gdpr_scc_register.csv
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval scc_age_days=round((now()-signed_epoch)/86400,0)
| lookup gdpr_processor_register.csv processor_name OUTPUT sub_processors_last_changed
| eval sub_change_epoch=strptime(sub_processors_last_changed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval review_needed=if(sub_change_epoch > signed_epoch OR scc_age_days > 1095, 1, 0)
| where review_needed=1 OR scc_version!="2021"
| table processor_name, scc_version, signed_date, scc_age_days, sub_processors_last_changed, review_needed
description: SCCs must be implemented and kept current with module and annex accuracy. This use case monitors SCC version, signing dates, linked processing activities, and triggers review when processor subprocessors or transfer countries change materially.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Record each SCC with version (2021 modules) and signing date; (2) sync sub-processor change dates from vendor notifications; (3) alert legal when sub-processor changes post-date the SCC signature; (4) schedule triennial SCC review tasks; (5) archive signed PDFs externally with URLs in register. Lookups: `gdpr_processor_register`, `gdpr_processor_register.csv`, `gdpr_scc_register`, `gdpr_scc_register.csv`.
visualization: Table (SCCs needing review), Single value (outdated SCC count), Timeline (signature vs sub-processor changes), Bar chart (by module type). Key fields: `processor_name`, `review_needed`, `scc_age_days`, `scc_version`, `signed_date`, `signed_epoch`.
knownFalsePositives: Transfers logged during pre-approved vendor agreements, standard contractual clauses in flight, or planned data migration windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `gdpr_scc_register.csv`, `gdpr_processor_register.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Record each SCC with version (2021 modules) and signing date; (2) sync sub-processor change dates from vendor notifications; (3) alert legal when sub-processor changes post-date the SCC signature; (4) schedule triennial SCC review tasks; (5) archive signed PDFs externally with URLs in register.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_scc_register.csv
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval scc_age_days=round((now()-signed_epoch)/86400,0)
| lookup gdpr_processor_register.csv processor_name OUTPUT sub_processors_last_changed
| eval sub_change_epoch=strptime(sub_processors_last_changed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval review_needed=if(sub_change_epoch > signed_epoch OR scc_age_days > 1095, 1, 0)
| where review_needed=1 OR scc_version!="2021"
| table processor_name, scc_version, signed_date, scc_age_days, sub_processors_last_changed, review_needed
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) Compliance Tracking (Art. 46(2)(c))** — SCCs must be implemented and kept current with module and annex accuracy. This use case monitors SCC version, signing dates, linked processing activities, and triggers review when processor subprocessors or transfer countries change materially.

Documented **Data sources**: `gdpr_scc_register.csv`, `gdpr_processor_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **signed_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **scc_age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sub_change_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **review_needed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where review_needed=1 OR scc_version!="2021"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: processor_name, review_needed, scc_age_days, scc_version, signed_date, signed_epoch, sub_change_epoch, sub_processors_last_changed.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (SCCs needing review), Single value (outdated SCC count), Timeline (signature vs sub-processor changes), Bar chart (by module type).
grandmaExplanation: We sCCs must be implemented and kept current with module and annex accuracy so we can show we meet our privacy duties when people ask for proof.
